meta.sourceBoundary: Local sample data based on verified run receipts. No social platform writes.
meta.publishingBoundary: Drafts are private candidates until Clay manually reviews, edits, and posts.

narrative.whatChanged: Full-blast research converged on a private Build Signal OS instead of an auto-posting workflow.
narrative.whyItMattered: The system can make Clay's work visible without turning it into a content treadmill.
narrative.lesson: Proof, decision tension, and manual review are the durable center of the workflow.
narrative.nextStep: Build a local dashboard that turns signals into scored candidates and visual receipts.

narrative.whatChanged: Existing local automation already covered draft audits and content loop scouting.
narrative.whyItMattered: Trackoor should feed those systems with better personal build receipts, not duplicate them.
narrative.blocker: Some candidates rely on private local paths and are not publishable without redaction.
narrative.lesson: The missing layer is a ledger of signals, proof strength, and public-safe narrative arcs.
narrative.nextStep: Represent publication status and proof strength in the UI.

signals.redactionNotes: No raw trace or private transcript included.
signals.title: The workflow should be a candidate generator, not an auto-poster.
signals.whyItMatters: It keeps public output tied to evidence, judgment, and manual voice.

signals.title: Trackoor started as a blank repo with no commits.
signals.whyItMatters: The first public artifact can show the birth of the tool with accurate local-only status.

signals.redactionNotes: Local paths and private content state summarized only.
signals.title: The Content Engine scout already uses ship_candidate, needs_artifact, and retire_or_refresh.
signals.whyItMatters: Trackoor can reuse that editorial taxonomy for Clay's personal account.

signals.title: Vanta/private-readiness claims must stay blocked without fresh proof.
signals.whyItMatters: Personal posts can be interesting precisely because they show restraint and failed gates.

proofs.name: Full-blast research synthesis
proofs.summary: Five lanes converged on local-first tracking, manual-only publishing, proof-bound candidates, and visual artifact previews.

proofs.name: Trackoor repo preflight
proofs.summary: Repo existed on main with no commits and no project files before this prototype.

proofs.name: Paused dual X draft audit builder
proofs.summary: Existing local automation prepares draft packages only and preserves manual-only social boundaries.

proofs.name: Daily Content Engine practical-loop scout
proofs.summary: Existing scout classifies content runs as ship_candidate, needs_artifact, retire_or_refresh, or learned.

proofs.name: Vanta claim boundary
proofs.summary: No production, mainnet, audit, privacy-readiness, customer, or adoption claims without current receipts.

candidates.readyDefinition: Proof attached, copy risk acceptable, redaction passed, deployment truth accurate, and manual approval complete.
candidates.draft: I am building a local dashboard that turns my agent runs into public-safe build receipts. The trick is not more content. It is evidence, risk flags, and one manual decision: is this actually worth saying?
candidates.readerValue: Explains the product philosophy and gives other builders a reusable frame.
riskNotes: Keep as personal workflow, not a production product claim.
Do not mention private repo paths.

candidates.readyDefinition: Proof attached, copy risk acceptable, redaction passed, deployment truth accurate, and manual approval complete.
candidates.draft: The most useful public build artifact might be a tiny status ledger: local, committed, pushed, live. It forces every update to say what is real instead of implying the work has shipped farther than it has.
candidates.readerValue: Turns repo hygiene into a public trust primitive.
riskNotes: Never mark live without deployment proof.

candidates.readyDefinition: Blocked until a specific current proof packet supports the claim class.
candidates.draft: Not everything deserves a launch post. Some of the most honest progress is a failed gate: local prototype works, but production/privacy/readiness claims stay blocked until the receipts exist.
candidates.readerValue: Makes restraint feel like part of the build story.
riskNotes: Do not imply a specific Vanta feature is live.
Keep claim class generic unless a current source packet is attached.

candidates.readyDefinition: Rejected because it lacks a lesson, decision, or proof tension.
candidates.draft: Built a dashboard today. More soon.
candidates.readerValue: Too generic to carry real signal.
riskNotes: No lesson, decision, or proof tension.

artifacts.name: Lumi Ledger
artifacts.summary: A status strip that separates local work from committed, pushed, and live reality.

rows.next: Verify prototype, then decide whether to commit.
Resolve publication route before relying on it.

artifacts.name: Agent Work Storyboard
artifacts.summary: Four panels that make an agent run legible without exposing private traces.

panels.title: Goal
panels.body: Turn private build work into public-safe personal account material.

panels.title: Lanes
panels.body: Research, visual artifacts, capture schema, safety, and product shape.

panels.title: Finding
panels.body: The missing layer is not auto-posting. It is proof-bound candidate selection.

panels.title: Proof
panels.body: Local schema, UI, validation, and manual-only gates.

artifacts.name: Decision Constellation
artifacts.summary: A map of public claims, supporting proof, and blocked edges.

artifacts.name: Anti-Launch Card
artifacts.summary: A receipt for work that should not be promoted yet.
artifacts.gate: Blocked from production/privacy-readiness wording until fresh receipts exist.
artifacts.next: Attach a verified public-safe proof packet or keep as internal learning.

distributionLedger.learning: Track first; publish only after evidence and voice review.
